test_cases.input: Design a new interaction interface for the chating robot.

arguments.user_personas: Young adults (18-35) who frequently use messaging apps, prefer quick and intuitive UI, comfortable with emojis and GIFs.
arguments.interaction_modes: Voice commands, text chat, gesture recognition.
arguments.design_principles: Minimalist design, high contrast for readability, responsive layout, accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1).
arguments.usability_testing: A/B testing with 50 users, heuristic evaluation, think-aloud protocol.
arguments.output_artifacts: Interactive prototype, persona documentation, usability test report.

tools_called.name: UIUX_Expert

args.user_personas: Primary personas: busy knowledge workers (25-45) who need quick answers and task completion; students who need explanations and summarization; casual users who want friendly conversational assistance. Secondary personas: power users who need shortcuts, file handling, and multi-turn task workflows. Needs: fast onboarding, low-friction typing, clear response scanning, trust, accessibility, and support for both simple Q&A and richer task-oriented interactions.
args.interaction_modes: Text chat as the primary mode; voice input/output as an optional mode; quick-reply chips and suggested prompts for low-friction starts; attachment/file upload for documents and images; action buttons for follow-up tasks (regenerate, summarize, translate, copy, continue); keyboard shortcuts for power users; inline rich content cards for structured answers.
args.design_principles: Clarity first, minimal cognitive load, strong visual hierarchy, accessible contrast and spacing, responsive layout, conversational friendliness, action-oriented responses, progressive disclosure for advanced tools, error-tolerant flows, and persistent input visibility. The interface should make the next step obvious at all times and keep the chat readable even for long or complex responses.
args.prototyping_tools: Figma for wireframes and high-fidelity interactive prototypes; shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS for component-based implementation-ready previews; optional Framer for motion testing of typing/loading and message transitions.
args.usability_testing: Heuristic evaluation for chat clarity and discoverability; task-based usability tests for common flows (ask a question, upload a file, continue a thread, use a quick action); A/B testing for prompt chips, composer placement, and response action buttons; accessibility checks for keyboard navigation, screen-reader labels, focus states, and color contrast; telemetry review for drop-off after first message and success rate on suggested prompts.
args.output_artifacts: Information architecture outline, low-fidelity wireframe concept, component inventory, interaction state map (empty/loading/error/streaming), microcopy set, and a clickable prototype ready for stakeholder review.

Secondary personas: power users who need shortcuts, file handling, and multi-turn task workflows. Needs: fast onboarding, low-friction typing, clear response scanning, trust, accessibility, and support for both simple Q&A and richer task-oriented interactions.
input_parameters.interaction_modes: Text chat as the primary mode; voice input/output as an optional mode; quick-reply chips and suggested prompts for low-friction starts; attachment/file upload for documents and images; action buttons for follow-up tasks (regenerate, summarize, translate, copy, continue); keyboard shortcuts for power users; inline rich content cards for structured answers.
input_parameters.design_principles: Clarity first, minimal cognitive load, strong visual hierarchy, accessible contrast and spacing, responsive layout, conversational friendliness, action-oriented responses, progressive disclosure for advanced tools, error-tolerant flows, and persistent input visibility. The interface should make the next step obvious at all times and keep the chat readable even for long or complex responses.
input_parameters.prototyping_tools: Figma for wireframes and high-fidelity interactive prototypes; shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS for component-based implementation-ready previews; optional Framer for motion testing of typing/loading and message transitions.
input_parameters.usability_testing: Heuristic evaluation for chat clarity and discoverability; task-based usability tests for common flows (ask a question, upload a file, continue a thread, use a quick action); A/B testing for prompt chips, composer placement, and response action buttons; accessibility checks for keyboard navigation, screen-reader labels, focus states, and color contrast; telemetry review for drop-off after first message and success rate on suggested prompts.
input_parameters.output_artifacts: Information architecture outline, low-fidelity wireframe concept, component inventory, interaction state map (empty/loading/error/streaming), microcopy set, and a clickable prototype ready for stakeholder review.

test_cases.input: Please provide a website development proposal, including website architecture, database design, and user experience optimization.

arguments.system_design: A three-tier website architecture with a presentation layer (React.js frontend), an application layer (Node.js/Express backend), and a data layer (PostgreSQL). Incorporates CDN and load balancer for scalability and performance, along with secure API endpoints and modular components for maintainability.
arguments.technology_stack: Frontend: React.js, Backend: Node.js with Express, Database: PostgreSQL, Hosting: AWS EC2 & S3, CDN: CloudFront, Version Control: GitHub
arguments.hardware_requirements: Cloud-based hosting with minimum 2 vCPUs, 8GB RAM for app servers, AWS RDS for database, and 50GB SSD storage; scalable load balancer configuration for peak loads.
arguments.interaction_interface: Responsive web interface with mobile-first design, intuitive navigation, accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1), and optimized load times; includes interactive UI elements and seamless database-driven content updates.
arguments.test_optimization_plan: Automated unit and integration testing using Jest and Cypress, load testing with Apache JMeter, continuous performance monitoring via New Relic, and iterative A/B testing for UI/UX refinement.

tools_called.name: system_architecture_designer

args.system_design: Design a scalable, secure, content-driven website architecture suitable for a modern business website with marketing pages, blog/news content, user accounts, and optional e-commerce or service inquiry workflows. The system should separate presentation, application logic, and data access layers; support role-based access control; include APIs for front-end consumption; and be easy to extend for future features such as search, personalization, and analytics.
args.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js with React and TypeScript; styling via Tailwind CSS or a design system such as Chakra UI. Backend: Node.js with NestJS or Next.js API routes for modular services; REST or GraphQL API. Database: PostgreSQL as the primary relational database; Redis for caching/session storage; object storage for media assets (e.g., S3-compatible). Infrastructure: Docker for containerization, CI/CD via GitHub Actions, hosting on AWS/Vercel/Render depending on scale, CDN for static assets, and observability with logging/metrics tools.
args.hardware_requirements: For a small-to-medium production deployment: 2–4 vCPU application instances, 4–8 GB RAM per instance, SSD-backed storage, managed PostgreSQL with at least 2 vCPU/4–8 GB RAM to start, Redis with 1 vCPU/1–2 GB RAM, and scalable object storage for images/files. If traffic is expected to grow, use autoscaling app instances, managed database read replicas, and CDN caching to reduce origin load.
args.interaction_interface: Responsive web UI optimized for desktop and mobile. Include clear navigation, search, forms with validation, authentication flows, admin dashboard for content management, and accessible components that meet WCAG guidelines. Prioritize fast page loads and intuitive interaction patterns.
args.test_optimization_plan: Implement unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for core flows; use automated linting and type checking in CI; set performance budgets for Core Web Vitals; monitor error rates and response times; run A/B tests on key UX changes; collect user feedback through analytics and surveys; and iterate on bottlenecks using profiling and load testing.
